title: jumellea lignosa subsp. lignosa
intro: Jumellea lignosa subsp. lignosa, also known as the Vanilla orchid, is a flowering plant species native to Madagascar, characterized by its unique, woody stem and fragrant, cream-colored flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Jumellea lignosa subsp. lignosa include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the flowers or roots in hot water, and the resulting tea can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
